---
title: Block 3
---
```{r, echo = FALSE, results='asis'}
library(webexercises)
```

## Differentialrechnung

### Übungen 3 – A1
Bestimmen Sie die Ableitung zu

::: {.webex-check .webex-box}
```{r, results='asis', echo=FALSE}
opts <- c(
  answer = "$f^{\\prime}(x) = 12x^{3} + 2x$",
  "$f^{\\prime}(x) = 12x^{3} + x$",
  "$f^{\\prime}(x) = 3x^{3} + 2x$"
)
cat("(a) ${f}(x) = 3 x^{4} + x^{2} + 5$",
  longmcq(opts))
```
:::

::: {.callout-tip collapse="true" title="Lösung"}
(a) $\\f(x) =  3x^{4} + x^{2} + 5 \Rightarrow f'(x) = 12x^{3} = 2x$
:::

::: {.webex-check .webex-box}
```{r, results='asis', echo=FALSE}
opts <- c(
	"$f^{\\prime}(x) = 2e^{3x}$",
	"$f^{\\prime}(x) = e^{3x}/3$",
	answer = "$f^{\\prime}(x) = 3e^{3x}$"
)

cat("(b) ${f}(x) = e^{3x}$",
	longmcq(opts))
```
:::

::: {.callout-tip collapse="true" title="Lösung"}
(b) $\\f(x) = e^{3x} \Rightarrow f'(x) = 3e^{3x}$
:::

::: {.webex-check .webex-box}
```{r, results='asis', echo=FALSE}
opts <- c(
	"$f^{\\prime}(x) = e^{3x} + 2 + 1$",
	answer = "$f^{\\prime}(x) = 3e^{3x} + 3$",
	"$f^{\\prime}(x) = e^{3x} + 3$"
)

cat("(c) ${f}(x) = 2x + e^{3x} + x$",
	longmcq(opts))
```
:::

::: {.callout-tip collapse="true" title="Lösung"}
(c) $\\f(x) = 2x + e^{3x} + x \Rightarrow f'(x) = 2 +3e^{3x} + 1 = 3e^{3x} + 3$
:::

::: {.webex-check .webex-box}
```{r, results='asis', echo=FALSE}
opts <- c(
	"$f^{\\prime}(x)  = 2 \\cdot (1 - x^{2} )^{-\\frac{1}{2}} \\cdot (-2x$)",
	"$f^{\\prime}(x) = 1 - 2x$",
	answer = "$f^{\\prime}(x)  = 1/2 \\cdot (1 - x^{2} )^{-\\frac{1}{2}} \\cdot (-2x$)"
)

cat("(d) ${f}(x) = \\sqrt{1 - x^{2}}$",
	longmcq(opts))
```
:::

::: {.callout-tip collapse="true" title="Lösung"}
(d) $\\f(x) = \sqrt{1 - x^{2}} = (1 - x^{2})^{1/2} \Rightarrow f'(x) = 1/2 \cdot (1 - x^{2})^{- \frac{1}{2}} \cdot (-2x)$
:::

::: {.webex-check .webex-box}
```{r, results='asis', echo=FALSE}
opts <- c(
	"$f^{\\prime}(x)$ = $e^{x}$ ($\\frac{1}{x} + \\ln(x))$",
	answer = "$f^{\\prime}(x)$  = $e^{x}$ $\\cdot$ $\\frac{1}{x}$ + $e^{x}$ $\\cdot \\ln(x)$",
	"$f^{\\prime}(x) = e^{x} \\cdot \\frac{1}{x}$"
)

cat("(e) ${f}(x) = e^{x} \\cdot ln(x)$",
	longmcq(opts))
```
::: 

::: {.callout-tip collapse="true" title="Lösung"}
(e) $\\f(x) = e^{x} \cdot \ln(x) \Rightarrow f'(x) = e^{x} \cdot \frac{1}{x} + e^{x} \cdot \ln(x)$
:::

::: {.webex-check .webex-box}
```{r, results='asis', echo=FALSE}
opts <- c(
	answer = "$f^{\\prime}(x) = (x + 4) \\cdot (-2) \\cdot \\frac{1}{x^{3}} + 1 \\cdot \\frac{1}{x^{2}}$",
	"$f^{\\prime}(x) = (x + 4) \\cdot \\frac{1}{x^{3}} + 1 \\cdot \\frac{1}{x^{2}}$",
	"$f^{\\prime}(x) = \\frac{1}{x^{2}} + 1 \\cdot \\frac{1}{x^{3}}$"
)

cat("(f) ${f}(x) =  \\frac{(x+4)}{x^{2}}$",
	longmcq(opts))
```
:::

::: {.callout-tip collapse="true" title="Lösung"}
(f) $\\f(x) = \frac{ (x + 4) }{x^{2}} = (x + 4) \cdot \frac{1}{x^{2}} \Rightarrow f'(x) = (x + 4) \cdot (-2) \cdot \frac{1}{x^{3}} + 1 \cdot \frac{1}{x^{2}}$
:::

::: {.webex-check .webex-box}
```{r, results='asis', echo=FALSE}
opts <- c(
	"$f^{\\prime}(x) = (x + 4) \\cdot e^{x}$",
	"$f^{\\prime}(x) = 1 + e^{x}$",
	answer = "$f^{\\prime}(x) = (x + 4) \\cdot e^{x} + 1 \\cdot e^{x}$"
)

cat("(g) ${f}(x) = (x + 4) \\cdot e^{x}$",
	longmcq(opts))
```
:::

::: {.callout-tip collapse="true" title="Lösung"}
(g) $\\f(x) = (x + 4) \cdot e^{x} \Rightarrow f'(x) = (x + 4) \cdot e^{x} + 1 \cdot e^{x}$
:::

::: {.webex-check .webex-box}
```{r, results='asis', echo=FALSE}
opts <- c(
	answer = "$f^{\\prime}(x) = 1/(x - 4)$",
	"$f^{\\prime}(x) = x - 4$",
	"$f^{\\prime}(x) = \\frac{1}{x^{x}} + 2$"
)

cat("(h) ${f}(x) = ln(x - 4) + 2$",
	longmcq(opts))
```
:::

::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"}
(h) $\\f(x) = \\ln(x - 4) + 2 \Rightarrow f'(x) = 1/(x - 4)$
:::

::: {.webex-check .webex-box}
```{r, results='asis', echo=FALSE}
opts <- c(
	"$f^{\\prime}(x) = e^{-2x}$",
	answer = "$f^{\\prime}(x) = -2e^{-2x}$",
	"$f^{\\prime}(x) = 2e^{-2x}$"
)

cat("(i) ${f}(x) = e^{-2x}$",
	longmcq(opts))
```
:::

::: {.callout-tip collapse="true" title="Lösung"}
(i) $\\f(x) = e^{-2x} \Rightarrow f'(x) = -2e^{-2x}$
:::

::: {.webex-check .webex-box}
```{r, results='asis', echo=FALSE}
opts <- c(
	answer = "$f^{\\prime}(x) = -4x \\cdot e^{-2x^{2}}$",
	"$f^{\\prime}(x) = e^{-2x^{2}}$",
	"$f^{\\prime}(x) = -2x \\cdot e^{-2x^{2}}$"
)

cat("(j) ${f}(x) = e^{-2x^{2}}$",
	longmcq(opts))
```
:::

::: {.callout-tip collapse="true" title="Lösung"}
(j) $\\f(x) = e^{-2x} \Rightarrow f'(x) = -4x \cdot e^{-2x^{2}}$
:::

### Übungen 3 – A2
Gegeben ist die Funktion ${f}(x) = 8 x^{3} + 3 x^{2} – 3x$.
<br><br> Verwenden Sie folgende Regel:<br> Nullstellen einer quadratischen Funktion $f(x) = ax^{2} + bx + c$ gegeben
durch: $x_{1,2} = \frac{-b \pm \sqrt{b^{2} - 4ac}}{2a}$ <br><br> 

::: {.webex-check .webex-box}
```{r, results='asis', echo=FALSE}
opts <- c(
	 answer = "$f^{\\prime}(x) = 24x^{2} + 6x - 3$",
  "$f^{\\prime}(x)= 16x^{2} + 6x - 3$",
  "$f^{\\prime}(x)= 24x^{2} + 6x + 3$"
)

cat(
"(a) Bestimmen Sie die erste Ableitung der Funktion ${f}(x) = 8 x^{3} + 3 x^{2} – 3x$.<br><br>",
longmcq(opts))
```
:::

::: {.callout-tip collapse="true" title="Lösung"}
(a) Zunächst muss die Ableitung gebildet werden.<br>
	$\\f'(x) = 24x^{2} + 6x -3$ <br>
:::

::: {.webex-check .webex-box}
```{r, results='asis', echo=FALSE}

cat("(b) Bestimmen Sie die Nullstellen der Ableitung sowie die lokalen Extremstellen der Funktion im Intervall $[-1,1]$.
Skizzieren Sie die Funktion zunächst auf dem Intervall $[-1,1]$.<br><br>",
"**Nullstellen der Ableitung:** <br><br>",
"$x_{1} =$ ", fitb(-0.5), "<br>",
"$x_{2} =$ ", fitb(0.25), "<br><br>",
"**Lokale Extremstellen:** <br><br>",
"$f(x_{1}) =$ ", fitb(1.25), "<br>",
"$f(x_{2}) =$ ", fitb(-0.4375), "<br>"
)
```
:::

::: {.callout-tip collapse="true" title="Lösung"}
(b) Von der Ableitung aus Teilaufgabe (a) müssen nun die Nullstellen bestimmt werden: <br><br>
	$\\0 = 24x^{2} + 6x - 3 \Leftrightarrow 0 = 8x^{2} + 2x -1$ <br><br>
	$\\ \Leftrightarrow x_{1,2} = \frac{-2 \pm \sqrt{4 - 4 \cdot 8 \cdot (-1)}}{16} = \frac{-2 \pm \sqrt{36}}{16}$ <br><br>
	Nullstellen der Ableitung:
	$$x_{1} = - 0.5$$ $$x_{2} = 0.25$$

	<img src="images/image_3A2.png" width="300">

	Eine Auswertung der Funktion an den möglichen Extremstellen ergibt:
	$$f(-0.5) = 1.25$$
	$$f(0.25) = -0.4375$$

	Damit liegen die lokalen Extrempunkte bei:
	$$(−0.5,1.25)$$ $$(0.25, −0.4375)$$

:::
